content:
Case 2:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 24
- Sex: female
- Chief complaint: New skin changes on the back and extremities
- Vital signs: BP 120/75 mmHg, HR 116/min, T° 36.8 °C, respiratory rate 16/min.
Setting: Outpatient/Day Unit (Initial Presentation)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient with new symptoms in an ambulatory or day-unit setting. You can perform a focused history and examination, obtain routine blood panels, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging as first-line tests. Invasive tissue sampling and specialized analyses usually require additional coordination and are not available in this round.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "focused physical examination detailing the characteristics of the skin changes, including color, morphology, distribution, presence of ulceration or lesions, and any associated systemic signs."}}

response.request: focused physical examination detailing the characteristics of the skin changes, including color, morphology, distribution, presence of ulceration or lesions, and any associated systemic signs.

content:
History Physical:
On June 4, 2025, the patient presented with new skin changes, first noticed by her partner on the back and now also on the extremities. She reported intermittent headaches and mild nausea, as well as chronic joint pain and stiffness. Otherwise, she was in good general condition, afebrile, and showed no signs of skin or mucosal GvHD. A marked psychological stress reaction was noted.
Current medication: pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20,000 IE 1×/week, calcium 500 mg 1-0-0, posaconazole 300 mg 1×/d, ruxolitinib 10 mg 1-0-1, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, co-trimoxazole 960 mg orally once daily, three times per week (Monday, Wednesday, Friday), steroid cream p.r.n.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "results of a focused skin examination describing the rash morphology, distribution, color, presence of scaling or ulceration, and any other notable skin findings."}}

response.request: results of a focused skin examination describing the rash morphology, distribution, color, presence of scaling or ulceration, and any other notable skin findings.

differentials: Drug-induced maculopapular rash (e.g., caused by posaconazole, cotrimoxazole, or valaciclovir)
Dermatomyositis or other idiopathic inflammatory myopathy

treatment_plan.action: Initiate high-dose systemic corticosteroid therapy (prednisone 1 mg/kg PO daily or equivalent)
Reassess ruxolitinib dosing (consider increasing dose or adding steroid-sparing agent)
Start supportive skin care (moisturizers, topical corticosteroid cream, antihistamines)
Order basic cardiac workup including ECG and repeat troponin/NT-proBNP with possible initiation of diuretics and ACEi/ARB if indicated for heart failure

response.request: results of a punch skin biopsy (histopathology plus immunofluorescence if performed) to evaluate for cutaneous graft‑versus‑host disease or drug‑induced dermatitis.

content:
Skin Biopsy:
Skin biopsy (2025-06-07): The dermis and subcutis are diffusely infiltrated by atypical hematopoietic cells in a perivascular and interstitial distribution. The cells show irregular nuclear contours, vesicular chromatin, and occasional nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent. Immunohistochemistry: CD34-, CD33+, MPO+, Lysozyme and CD68+, CD117+, negative for CD20-, CD19-, CD3-. Molecular pathology: NPM1 mutation present in cutaneous infiltration.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow aspirate and core biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and molecular studies to confirm systemic acute myeloid leukemia and assess for additional cytogenetic abnormalities."}}

response.request: bone marrow aspirate and core biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and molecular studies to confirm systemic acute myeloid leukemia and assess for additional cytogenetic abnormalities.

content:
Bone Marrow:
June 5, 2025: Morphology: Normocellular bone marrow with age-appropriate cellularity. Trilineage hematopoiesis present with some dysmorphic maturation in erythroid, myeloid, and megakaryocytic lineages, consistent with reactive changes after alloSCT. No excess blasts or abnormal infiltrates were observed.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "whole-body PET-CT with contrast to identify the extent of extramedullary disease and any additional occult lesions."}}

response.request: whole-body PET-CT with contrast to identify the extent of extramedullary disease and any additional occult lesions.

content:
Pet Ct:
FDG-PET/CT (2025-06-07): Revealed multiple FDG-avid cutaneous lesions (SUVmax 6.2) and intra-abdominal lesions adjacent to the left ureter (SUVmax 8.1), accompanied by several mildly FDG-avid mediastinal lymph nodes (SUVmax 4.5).

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Extramedullary relapse of acute myeloid leukemia (leukemia cutis) following allogeneic stem cell transplant", "differentials": ["Cutaneous graft\u2011versus\u2011host disease", "Drug\u2011induced maculopapular dermatitis (e.g., from posaconazole, cotrimoxazole, valaciclovir)", "Other inflammatory skin disorders such as dermatitis or psoriasis"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate induction chemotherapy with a 7+3 regimen (cytarabine continuous 100 mg/m\u00b2 for 7 days + daunorubicin 60 mg/m\u00b2 IV daily for 3 days)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Add venetoclax (if fit) at 400 mg daily with dose ramp\u2011up in combination with cytarabine and anthracycline", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Administer supportive transfusion and growth factor support (erythropoietin or G\u2011CSF) and broad\u2011spectrum antimicrobial prophylaxis", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Order donor\u2011chimerism testing and consider a second allogeneic transplant if salvage therapy fails", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Apply localized external\u2011beam radiation to symptomatic cutaneous lesions to provide palliation", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Extramedullary relapse of acute myeloid leukemia (leukemia cutis) following allogeneic stem cell transplant
differentials: Drug‑induced maculopapular dermatitis (e.g., from posaconazole, cotrimoxazole, valaciclovir)
Other inflammatory skin disorders such as dermatitis or psoriasis

treatment_plan.action: Initiate induction chemotherapy with a 7+3 regimen (cytarabine continuous 100 mg/m² for 7 days + daunorubicin 60 mg/m² IV daily for 3 days)
Add venetoclax (if fit) at 400 mg daily with dose ramp‑up in combination with cytarabine and anthracycline
Administer supportive transfusion and growth factor support (erythropoietin or G‑CSF) and broad‑spectrum antimicrobial prophylaxis
Order donor‑chimerism testing and consider a second allogeneic transplant if salvage therapy fails
Apply localized external‑beam radiation to symptomatic cutaneous lesions to provide palliation

Setting: Inpatient Ward / Multidisciplinary Tumor Board (Definitive Characterization)
Capabilities: You now have access to results from specialized tests with longer turnaround times, including detailed pathology, molecular profiling, and measurable residual disease assessments, as well as a complete overview of prior therapies. You are expected to integrate these data into a definitive diagnosis, risk assessment, and treatment strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

content: {"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Extramedullary relapse of acute myeloid leukemia (leukemia cutis) with NPM1 mutation after allogeneic stem\u2011cell transplant", "differentials": ["Cutaneous graft\u2011versus\u2011host disease", "Drug\u2011induced maculopapular dermatitis", "Primary cutaneous lymphoma"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Administer salvage chemotherapy with FLAG\u2011IDA (fludarabine 30\u202fmg/m\u00b2 days\u202f1\u20113, cytarabine 2\u202fg/m\u00b2 days\u202f1\u20113, idarubicin 12\u202fmg/m\u00b2 days\u202f1\u20113, G\u2011CSF 300\u202fIU/m\u00b2 days\u202f1\u20113) plus venetoclax 400\u202fmg daily (dose ramp\u2011up) for 14 cycles", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Perform donor chimerism analysis and, if remission achieved, proceed to a second allogeneic transplant (matched related or unrelated donor) within 2\u20134 weeks", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Administer localized external\u2011beam radiation (e.g., 30\u202fGy in 10 fractions) to symptomatic cutaneous lesions for local control and palliation", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Supportive care: transfusion support, G\u2011CSF, prophylactic antibacterial and antifungal therapy, monitor for infections", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Re\u2011evaluate response with PET\u2011CT after 3 cycles of salvage therapy and bone\u2011marrow aspirate with flow cytometry for measurable residual disease", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Extramedullary relapse of acute myeloid leukemia (leukemia cutis) with NPM1 mutation after allogeneic stem‑cell transplant

treatment_plan.action: Administer salvage chemotherapy with FLAG‑IDA (fludarabine 30 mg/m² days 1‑3, cytarabine 2 g/m² days 1‑3, idarubicin 12 mg/m² days 1‑3, G‑CSF 300 IU/m² days 1‑3) plus venetoclax 400 mg daily (dose ramp‑up) for 14 cycles
Perform donor chimerism analysis and, if remission achieved, proceed to a second allogeneic transplant (matched related or unrelated donor) within 2–4 weeks
Administer localized external‑beam radiation (e.g., 30 Gy in 10 fractions) to symptomatic cutaneous lesions for local control and palliation
Supportive care: transfusion support, G‑CSF, prophylactic antibacterial and antifungal therapy, monitor for infections
Re‑evaluate response with PET‑CT after 3 cycles of salvage therapy and bone‑marrow aspirate with flow cytometry for measurable residual disease
